title: Golden Visa for Outstanding Students
eligibility: High school students with top grades nationwide (min 95%) or university graduates from top UAE/global universities with a GPA of at least 3.5 or 3.8 depending on classification.
documents: Academic transcripts, graduation certificate attested by Ministry of Education, Emirates ID copy.
steps: 1. Apply for eligibility nomination via ICP or GDRFA. 2. Submit academic records for verification. 3. Upon approval, apply for the 10-year residence visa online.
fees: Approx. AED 2,800 to AED 3,800 (Subject to change)

title: Student Visa Residency Requirements
eligibility: Expatriate students studying at an accredited university or college in the UAE. Must be sponsored by an official educational institution or a parent.
documents: Official admission letter, medical fitness certificate, passport copy, visa photo, sponsor's documents.
steps: 1. Receive admission letter. 2. Register for visa sponsorship through the university administration. 3. Pass medical fitness test. 4. Apply for Emirates ID and visa stamping.
fees: Approx. AED 1,000 to AED 3,000 depending on university/emirate (Subject to change)

title: Tourist Visa and Standard Visit Extensions
eligibility: Tourists from non-visa-on-arrival countries. Must be sponsored by a UAE travel agency, airline, or hotel.
documents: Passport copy (valid min 6 months), passport-size photograph, confirmed return flight ticket.
steps: 1. Apply via travel agent, hotel, or ICP/GDRFA portals before arrival. 2. Receive electronic entry permit. 3. Extend within the country via ICP application if needed.
fees: Approx. AED 300 to AED 600 depending on duration (30/60 days) (Subject to change)

title: Job Explorer Visa Eligibility
eligibility: Graduates from the top 500 universities in the world (Ministry of Education classification), holding a bachelor's degree or equivalent, or workers in the 1st, 2nd, or 3rd skill levels of MOHRE.
documents: Attested degree certificate, passport copy, clear passport photo, financial guarantee deposit.
steps: 1. Log into ICP smart services portal. 2. Select Job Explorer Visa (60, 90, or 120 days). 3. Upload documents and pay security deposit + fees.
fees: Approx. AED 1,000 to AED 1,500 including deposit (Subject to change)

title: Green Visa for Skilled Employees
eligibility: Freelancers, self-employed individuals, or skilled employees. Requires a valid employment contract, bachelor's degree, and a minimum monthly salary of AED 15,000.
documents: MOHRE employment contract, attested degree certificate, bank statements showing salary history.
steps: 1. Apply on ICP or GDRFA portal. 2. Upload proof of skill level and income tracking. 3. Cancel previous visa if internal change of status is needed.

title: Overstay Fines and Grace Periods
eligibility: Any tourist, visitor, or resident who remains in the UAE after their visa validity or official grace period has expired.
documents: Passport copy, unified number (UID), expired visa copy.
steps: 1. Check fines online via ICP or GDRFA portal. 2. Pay fines online or at immigration offices/airports prior to departure.
fees: Standardized at AED 50 per day of overstaying (Subject to change)

title: Sponsoring Family Members (Dependent Visa)
eligibility: Residents (employers or employees) with a minimum salary of AED 4,000 or AED 3,000 plus accommodation. Can sponsor spouse and unmarried daughters of any age, and sons up to 25.
documents: Attested marriage certificate, attested birth certificates, registered tenancy contract, salary certificate.
steps: 1. Apply for entry permit for family. 2. Family enters UAE or performs status change. 3. Medical fitness test for members over 18. 4. Complete Emirates ID and residency stamping.
fees: Approx. AED 1,500 to AED 2,500 per dependent excluding deposits (Subject to change)

title: Driving License Conversion for Foreign License Holders
eligibility: Citizens of approved countries (e.g., GCC, UK, US, Canada, Australia, specific EU states) holding a valid driving license from their home country and a valid UAE residency visa.
documents: Original foreign driving license, legal translation of license (if not in English/Arabic), eye test certificate, passport copy, Emirates ID copy.
steps: 1. Complete an eye test at an authorized center. 2. Visit an RTA center (Dubai) or local licensing authority. 3. Submit documents, pay fees, and receive converted UAE driving license instantly.
fees: Approx. AED 850 to AED 1,000 (Subject to change)

title: Opening a New Driving License File
eligibility: UAE residents aged 18 or above who do not hold a license from an exempt/convertible country.
documents: Emirates ID copy, eye test results, no-objection certificate (NOC) if required by your emirate category.
steps: 1. Register at an authorized driving school. 2. Complete theoretical lectures and pass the theory test. 3. Complete practical training hours. 4. Pass internal and city road tests.
fees: Approx. AED 4,500 to AED 8,000 depending on training school (Subject to change)
processing_time: 2 to 3 months depending on test availability

title: Mainland Business License (DED)
eligibility: Individuals or corporations intending to conduct commercial, professional, or industrial business operations anywhere within the UAE mainland.
documents: Trade name reservation certificate, initial approval certificate, memorandum of association (MOA), lease agreement (Ejari), passports of partners.
steps: 1. Select business activity and legal form. 2. Register trade name at the Department of Economy and Tourism (DED). 3. Apply for initial approval. 4. Draft MOA and rent an office space. 5. Collect license.
fees: Approx. AED 10,000 to AED 25,000+ depending on activities and location (Subject to change)

title: Free Zone Business Setup
eligibility: Foreign investors wanting 100% ownership, tax exemptions, and zero customs duty without needing a mainland office footprint.
documents: Passport copies of shareholders, proof of address, business plan summary, application forms unique to the specific Free Zone Authority.
steps: 1. Choose a specific Free Zone (e.g., DAFZA, JAFZA, ADGM, Shams). 2. Submit application for name and activity approval. 3. Sign corporate registry documents. 4. Open corporate bank account.
fees: Approx. AED 5,000 to AED 20,000 registration packages (Subject to change)

title: Professional Services and Artisans License
eligibility: Professionals, consultants, or artisans whose business relies on intellectual or physical skills (e.g., IT consultancy, legal, design). Allows 100% foreign ownership with a local service agent.
documents: Passport copy, academic degrees or qualification certificates relevant to the profession, initial approval paperwork.
steps: 1. Reserve trade name at DED. 2. Sign a Local Service Agent (LSA) agreement. 3. Establish a physical office or virtual desk contract. 4. Pay fees and issue professional license.
fees: Approx. AED 8,000 to AED 15,000 (Subject to change)

title: Emirates ID Linkage and Verification
eligibility: All citizens and residents of the UAE must hold a valid Emirates ID issued by the ICP.
documents: Valid passport, valid residency visa or entry permit, original birth certificate for minors.
steps: 1. Fill out form via ICP website or typing center. 2. Pay fees. 3. Receive SMS for biometric data capture (fingerprints/photo) at an ICP center. 4. Collect card via post.
fees: Approx. AED 100 per year of visa validity + processing fees (Subject to change)

title: Residency Visa Renewal Timeline
eligibility: Current residency holders who wish to extend their stay under the same sponsor before or within the grace period after expiry.
documents: Sponsored passport (valid > 6 months), sponsor's Emirates ID, new medical fitness test, health insurance card.
steps: 1. Undergo medical screening. 2. Renew health insurance policy. 3. File renewal request via ICP or GDRFA portal. 4. Print updated virtual visa / receive new Emirates ID.
fees: Approx. AED 500 to AED 1,200 (Subject to change)

title: Residency Visa Cancellation Process
eligibility: Sponsors or employers who intend to terminate an individual's residency status owing to job resignation, termination, or leaving the country.
documents: Cancellation form signed by sponsor, original Emirates ID or police report if lost, labor clearance from MOHRE.
steps: 1. Employer submits cancellation to MOHRE. 2. Settlement of all labor dues. 3. Application forwarded to ICP/GDRFA for final cancellation. 4. Grace period starts.
fees: Approx. AED 150 to AED 300 (Subject to change)
